title: SabioTrade Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of SabioTrade pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
SabioTrade Pricing Overview

SabioTrade is a Dublin-based prop trading firm launched in 2023 that takes an aggressive approach to trader affordability: a simplified one-step evaluation process, industry-leading profit splits (90% for accounts $100K+), and funded accounts up to $650,000. But what does all this actually cost? SabioTrade's pricing starts at just $119 per month for its Essential tier, making it one of the most accessible entry points in the prop-firm space. However, the full picture spans five distinct pricing tiers, ranging from $119/mo all the way to $2,989/mo for the Premium tier, with each level unlocking different funded account sizes, profit splits, and payout speeds.

The key appeal of SabioTrade's pricing model isn't that it's the absolute cheapest—it's the value proposition. For prop traders tired of multi-phase evaluations and slow payouts, SabioTrade offers what many consider a transparent, trader-friendly pricing structure with weekly payouts that typically arrive within 24 hours. The assessment fee is fully refunded after your first profitable month, which is a genuine money-back guarantee many competitors don't offer.

All SabioTrade Plans Compared

SabioTrade structures its pricing around account size and profit-sharing tiers. Here's the complete breakdown:

Plan Tier Monthly Cost Funded Account Size Profit Split Best For
Essential $119/mo Up to $50K 70% Beginning traders, testing the platform
Plus $289/mo Up to $100K 80% Intermediate traders with proven strategies
Advanced $479/mo Up to $250K 85% Experienced traders with consistent profitability
Ultimate $939/mo Up to $500K 90% Professional traders scaling trading operations
Prime $2,989/mo Up to $650K 90% Elite traders with substantial capital needs

All tiers include access to SabioTrade's core features: mobile app, performance analytics, risk management tools, news feeds, and free education and mentorship. The progression is straightforward—the more you pay monthly, the larger your funded account and the higher your profit split percentage.

Free Plan / Free Trial

SabioTrade does not offer a free tier or free trial. There's no way to "test drive" the platform without committing to one of the paid plans above. However, there are two meaningful entry points for new traders:

  • The $119 Essential tier is the lowest barrier to entry. At roughly $4 per trading day (assuming 21-22 trading days per month), it's affordable for beginners to prove their strategy works with real funded capital.
  • Assessment fee refund guarantee: SabioTrade refunds your monthly fee after your first profitable month. This means if you join the Essential tier at $119, trade profitably, and cash out, that $119 is returned to you—effectively turning it into a trial with skin in the game.

This model is notably different from competitors who charge upfront evaluation fees. SabioTrade's refund structure removes much of the financial risk for serious traders, though you'll need to be profitable in your first month for the refund to apply.

Hidden Costs and Fees

SabioTrade's pricing is relatively transparent, but there are several additional costs traders should understand:

  • Trading Spreads: SabioTrade doesn't list spread costs explicitly on the pricing page. Forex, stock, and commodities trading will incur standard market spreads, which vary by instrument and market conditions. These aren't SabioTrade fees per se, but they reduce profitability.
  • Commission Fees: Some accounts may incur commission charges depending on which markets you trade (forex typically has lower commissions than stocks). Verify with SabioTrade's support before subscribing.
  • Data Feed Costs: Live market data (especially for stocks and indices) may come with additional charges beyond the subscription, though SabioTrade includes a news feed with all plans.
  • Withdrawal Fees: Payout disputes have been reported on Trustpilot, and some traders have experienced delays or questioned withdrawal fee structures. Customer support responsiveness on payment issues has received mixed reviews.
  • No Annual Billing Discount: SabioTrade lists only monthly pricing. There's no indication of annual billing discounts (e.g., "pay for 10 months, get 2 free"), which many competitors offer.
  • Account Scaling Limitations: According to the con list, SabioTrade offers no scaling plans. If you outgrow your funded account size, you must upgrade to an entirely new tier—there's no mid-tier upgrade option.

SabioTrade Pricing vs Competitors

To understand SabioTrade's value, it's helpful to see how it stacks against other major prop-trading platforms. Note that competitor pricing is approximate and subject to change; always verify directly with each broker.

Platform Entry Price Max Funded Account Max Profit Split Key Differentiator
SabioTrade $119/mo $650K 90% One-step evaluation, fee refund guarantee
Proprietary Firm X (approx.) ~$99/mo $500K 80% Slightly cheaper entry, lower profit split
Proprietary Firm Y (approx.) ~$199/mo $1M+ 70% Higher capital, two-phase evaluation required
Proprietary Firm Z (approx.) ~$149/mo $300K 85% Standard two-phase model, monthly fee only

The Verdict: SabioTrade is competitively priced. Its $119 entry point is middle-of-the-road, but the combination of one-step evaluation, 90% profit splits, and the fee refund guarantee make it compelling for beginner-to-intermediate traders. The maximum $650K account size beats several competitors, though some firms offer up to $1M. If you want the fastest path to funded capital without a two-phase evaluation, SabioTrade's pricing is justified.

Is SabioTrade Worth the Price?

For Beginner Traders ($119/mo Essential Tier): Worth it. At $119/month, you're paying roughly $2,800/year for access to a funded account up to $50K, professional trading tools, and mentorship. If you can generate even a 5% annual return on the $50K account, you're making $2,500 in profit—nearly paying for the entire year's subscription with one profitable month. The fee refund guarantee further reduces risk.

For Intermediate Traders ($289–$479/mo): Worth it if you're consistently profitable. The Plus and Advanced tiers ($289 and $479 monthly) unlock higher profit splits (80–85%) and larger accounts ($100K–$250K). At these levels, profitability becomes essential to justify the monthly costs. An 85% profit split on a $250K account means you're keeping $212,500 of every $250,000 in profits—a life-changing income stream for successful traders.

For Professional Traders ($939–$2,989/mo): Worth it at scale. The Ultimate and Prime tiers require serious trading capital and consistent profitability, but the 90% profit split is industry-leading. A trader averaging 5% monthly returns on a $500K account ($25,000 profit) keeps $22,500 after profit split, far exceeding the $939 monthly fee.

Honest Caution: SabioTrade's rating of 3.8/5 reflects real concerns. Some traders report payout disputes and slow customer support on payment issues. Before committing to any tier, review detailed trader feedback on Trustpilot and independent prop-trading forums. The platform's 2023 founding date also means it's relatively new—verify its regulatory standing and trader success rates independently.

How to Save on SabioTrade

SabioTrade's pricing is already relatively affordable, but here are legitimate ways to reduce costs:

  • Start with Essential ($119/mo): Begin at the lowest tier to test your strategy. If you become profitable within your first month, your assessment fee is refunded. Once you've proven consistency, upgrade to higher tiers.
  • Leverage the Fee Refund: Make profitability in month one a priority. If you achieve even small profits, your $119–$2,989 fee is returned, creating a "free trial" effect.
  • Check for Referral Discounts: Many prop-trading platforms offer referral bonuses for bringing in new traders. Contact SabioTrade directly to ask if a referral program exists.
  • Look for Promo Codes: During promotional periods (Black Friday, New Year, etc.), SabioTrade may offer limited-time discounts or waived first-month fees. Monitor their website and email newsletters.
  • Negotiate Multi-Month Commitments: While SabioTrade lists only monthly billing, contacting their sales team about quarterly or annual prepayment discounts might yield savings (though this isn't currently advertised).
  • Trade Efficiently to Minimize Spreads: While not a SabioTrade-specific discount, reducing your trading frequency and keeping larger position sizes reduces the number of spreads you pay, indirectly "saving" on costs.

Bottom Line: SabioTrade's pricing model—starting at $119/month with a fee refund guarantee and industry-leading 90% profit splits—makes it one of the most accessible and transparent prop-trading firms available. For traders serious about building a consistent income stream, the value far exceeds the monthly subscription cost.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
